sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 록히드마틴은 3분기 매출 186억 달러와 역대 최대 수주잔고 1,790억 달러를 기록하며 견조한 실적을 달성했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 3분기 매출 186억 달러를 기록했으며, 역대 최대 규모인 1,790억 달러의 수주잔고를 확보했습니다.
주주 환원 정책을 강화하여 분기 배당금을 5% 인상하고, 자사주 매입 승인 한도를 20억 달러 증액했습니다.

businessChanges.text: 미국 및 동맹국의 전례 없는 수요에 대응하기 위해 주요 사업 부문의 생산 능력을 대폭 확대하고 있습니다.
F-35 전투기 18 및 19차 도입 계약을 체결했으며, 3분기까지 총 143대의 F-35를 인도했습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 영업활동 현금흐름은 37억 달러, 잉여현금흐름은 33억 달러로 전년 동기 대비 증가했습니다.
배당 및 자사주 매입을 통해 총 18억 달러를 주주에게 환원했습니다.

watchItems.text: 역대 최대 수주잔고가 향후 매출 인식으로 전환되는 속도와 생산 능력 확대에 따른 비용 효율성을 확인할 필요가 있습니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: EX-99.1 2 ex991q32025.htm EX-99.1 Document Exhibit 99.1 News Release Lockheed Martin Reports Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results • Sales of $18.6 billion • Net earnings of $1.6 billion, or $6.95 per share • Cash from operations of $3.7 billion and free cash flow of $3.3 billion • Returned $1.8 billion of cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases • Increased share repurchase authority by $2 billion to a total authorization of $9 billion • Increased quarterly dividend by 5% to $3.45 per share • Record backlog of $179 billion • Updates 2025 outlook BETHESDA, Md., Oct. 21, 2025 – Lockheed Martin Corporation [NYSE: LMT] today reported third quarter 20  Normalized numeric aliases: $18.6 billion = 186억 달러; $1.6 billion = 16억 달러; $3.7 billion = 37억 달러; $3.3 billion = 33억 달러; $1.8 billion = 18억 달러; $2 billion = 20억 달러; $9 billion = 90억 달러; $179 billion = 1,790억 달러; $18.6 = 18.6달러; $1.8 = 1.8달러; $17.1 = 17.1달러; $2 = 2달러; $9 = 9달러; $3.45 = 3.45달러; $179 = 179달러; $6.80 = 6.80달러.

rter of 2024. Cash from operations was $3.7 billion in the third quarter of 2025, compared to $2.4 billion in the third quarter of 2024. Free cash flow was $3.3 billion in the third quarter of 2025, compared to $2.1 billion in the third quarter of 2024. “Based on the effectiveness and reliability of our products and systems, strong demand from Lockheed Martin’s customers—both in the United States and among our allies—continues. As a result of this unprecedented demand, we are increasing production capacity significantly across a wide range of our lines of business,” said Lockheed Martin Chairman, President, and CEO Jim Taiclet. “Our record $179 billion backlog—more than two and a half years of sales—underscores the trust our customers place in us and underpins our company’  Normalized numeric aliases: $3.7 billion = 37억 달러; $2.4 billion = 24억 달러; $3.3 billion = 33억 달러; $2.1 billion = 21억 달러; $179 billion = 1,790억 달러; $3.7 = 3.7달러; $2.4 = 2.4달러; $3.3 = 3.3달러; $179 = 179달러; $2.1 = 2.1달러.

r Rotary and Mission Systems and Missiles and Fire Control businesses, respectively. Additionally, in close collaboration with our customers, we finalized the contracts covering Lots 18 and 19 of the F‑35 early in the fourth quarter. Lockheed Martin has delivered a record 143 F‑35 Lightning II jets through the end of the third quarter. "Looking ahead, we are investing aggressively in both new digital technologies and physical production capacity needed to meet the top defense priorities of the United States and its allies—and we are doing so in partnership with a number of leading technology partners, large and small. Major national‑defense initiatives like the Golden Dome for America demand proven leadership in a wide range of skills, including integrated air and missile defense, space warfare and highly secure command‑and‑control systems.
